revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Izuharahachimangu-jinja]][[ja:厳原八幡宮神社]][[ko:이즈하라 하치만구 신사]][[zh:嚴原八幡宮神社]] {{Expand Japanese|厳原八幡宮神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine | native_name = 厳原八幡宮神社 | image = Izuhara-hachimangu jinja Haiden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.2065|129.28925}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین (ایمپیراتور)|bn|সম্রাট ওজিন|cs|Ódžin|de|Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|es|Ōjin Tennō|eu|Ōjin enperadorea|fa|امپراتور اوجین|fi|Ōjin|fr|Ōjin|hr|Ōjin|id|Kaisar Ōjin|it|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|ka|ოძინი|ko|오진 천황|nl|Ojin|pl|Ōjin|pt|Ojin|ru|Император Одзин|sh|Car Ōjin|simple|Emperor Ōjin|sl|Cesar Odžin|sv|Ojin|th|จักรพรรดิโอจิง|tl|Emperador Ōjin|tr|Ōjin (imparator)|uk|Імператор Одзін|uz|Imperator Ōjin|vi|Thiên hoàng Ōjin|yo|Emperor Ōjin|zh_min_nan|Ôzin Thian-hông|zh_yue|應神天皇|zh|应神天皇|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | established = {'time': '+0677-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} }} {{nihongo|'''Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine'''|厳原八幡宮神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tsushima]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|fa|معبد هاچیمان|fr|Sanctuaire Hachiman|it|Santuario di Hachiman|ja|八幡宮|simple|Hachiman shrines|vi|Đền thờ thần Hachiman|zh|八幡宮|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} * {{ill|Q1656379|ltq=Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q845945|ltq=Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tsushima|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tsushima|ja|対馬島の式内社一覧|qid=Q11458212|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tsushima}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == image (P18) == * Izuhara-hachimangu jinja Haiden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * 신|mni|ꯍꯆꯤꯃꯥꯟ|my|ဟချိမန်း|pl|Hachiman|pt|Hachiman|ru|Хатиман|simple|Hachiman|sl|Hačiman|sv|Hachiman|tr|Hachiman|uk|Хачіман|uz|Hachiman|vi|Hachiman|wuu|八幡神|zh|八幡神|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20160306092955/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/680202.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183377 ** P3831: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 2083125 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5310005006584 == Commons category (P373) == * Izuharahachimangu-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Watatsumino Shrine|lt=Watatsumino Shrine|qid=Q135041548|1=Watatsumino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県対馬市厳原町中村', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q31395041|ltq=Q31395041|qid=Q31395041|1=Mount Shimizu}} == described at URL (P973) == * https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183377 {{commons category|Category:Izuharahachimangu-jinja}} [[JA:厳原八幡宮神社]] [[KO:이즈하라 하치만구 신사]] [[ZH:嚴原八幡宮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11410120}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Izuhara-hachimangu jinja Haiden.JPG|270px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|qid=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tsushima|Q991032|qid=Q991032|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}645-1 Aza Shimizuyama, Nakamura, Izuhara-chō |位置 = {{Coord|34|12|23.4|N|129|17|21.3|E|region:JP-42_scale:20000|display=inline,title|name=Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=Himegami}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} candidate of {{ill|lt=Tsushima Province|Q1054861|qid=Q1054861|1=Tsushima Province}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 6 ({{ill|lt=677|Q30622|qid=Q30622|1=677}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 15 (lunar calendar)|Q835516|qid=Q835516|lt=lunar August 15|1=August 15 (Lunisolar)}} |神事 = Shinkō rite
Hōjōe |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Izuhara
Hachimangū Shrine|float=center|caption=|relief=1|width=200}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:Izuhara-hachimangu-jinja, enkei-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimizuyama on left, precinct below right}}]] '''Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine''' (いづはらはちまんぐうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nakamura, Izuhara-chō, {{ill|lt=Tsushima|Q991032|qid=Q991032|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|qid=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of {{ill|lt=Tsushima Province|Q1054861|qid=Q1054861|1=Tsushima Province}}. Its official name is "Hachimangū Shrine" (without "Izuhara"), but to distinguish it from other Hachimangū/Hachiman shrines it is also called "Izuhara Hachimangū." Climbing the stone steps, the haiden appears beyond the rōmon (in photos, the roof visible behind the haiden is the honden); the plaque on the rōmon also reads "Hachimangū Shrine." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=Himegami}} * {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} === History === According to shrine tradition, on the return from {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} visited Shimizuyama on {{ill|lt=Tsushima|Q52051|qid=Q52051|1=Tsushima Island}}, judged it to be a mountain where divine spirit dwells, set up a sacred rock boundary on the summit, placed a sacred mirror and offerings, and worshiped the heavenly and earthly deities. It is said this became the origin when in {{ill|lt=677|Q30622|qid=Q30622|1=677}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 6), by imperial order, shrine buildings were erected at the foot of Shimizuyama and {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined'Expanded Tsushima Island Gazette' (ed. Tsushima Education Association), issued Sept. 16, 1973, p.519. These origins follow old traditions; currently the shrine office distributes 'Brief Origin of Hachimangū Shrine.' For updated treatment, see Hisae Nagatome, 'Tsushima Kokushi' vol.1 (ancient section), relevant parts such as "Myojin-taisha of Shikinaisha".. A copy of a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1476|Q6611|qid=Q6611|1=1476}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8) says "this shrine is the original mausoleum of Otokoyama in this province and divine spirit protecting the nation for ten-thousand generations." There is a folk tradition that it is also related to origins of {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|qid=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}, center of Hachiman worship, but these folk accounts are not adopted in the currently edited/published 'Brief Origin of Hachimangū Shrine'. On Tsushima there were Hachimangū in {{ill|lt=Kamiagata District|Q10868208|qid=Q10868208|1=Kamiagata district}} and {{ill|lt=Shimoagata District|Q10868678|qid=Q10868678|1=Shimoagata district}}; the former (now {{ill|lt=Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099|qid=Q11559099|1=Kaijin Shrine}}) was called Kamitsu Hachimangū, and this shrine in Shimoagata was called Shimotsu Hachimangū. Around the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, it came to be called Fuchū Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4), this shrine was once renamed '''Watatsumi Shrine''' by identifying it with the Engishiki myojin-taisha "Watatsumi Shrine," but in {{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|qid=Q7832|1=1890}} (Meiji 23) it returned to the former Hachimangū name and became "Hachimangū Shrine," and today does not claim relation with Watatsumi ShrineEven as a Watatsumi candidate shrine, ritual continuity with Hachimangū is not present, so later denial may have been proper. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Oto Watatsumi Shrine|Q134930328|qid=Q134930328|1=Otowatatsumi Shrine}} (Kuwa, Izuhara-chō), which also currently does not claim Shikinaisha status.. It was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), and promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} in {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|qid=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5). === Gallery === File:Izuhara-hachimangu-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Izuhara-hachimangu-jinja, shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Izuhara-hachimangu-jinja, torii-1.jpg|Torii File:Izuhara-hachimangu-jinja, Hirano-jinja.jpg|Hirano Shrine in precinct File:Izuhara-hachimangu-jinja, Unoto-jinja.jpg|Unoto Shrine in precinct File:Izuhara-hachimangu-jinja, Tenjin-jinja.jpg|Tenjin Shrine in precinct === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * 'Tsushima Jinja-shi', Tsushima Shrine Parishioners Association, Nagasaki, 1966. NDL Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3451609 (accessed 2025-06-09) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} * {{ill|lt=Kaijin Shrine|Q11559099|qid=Q11559099|1=Kaijin Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefectural Tsushima Museum of History and Folklore|Q18337566|qid=Q18337566|1=Nagasaki Prefectural Tsushima Museum of History and Folklore}} === External links === {{commonscat|Izuharahachimangu-jinja}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/680101.html Watatsumi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Izuhara Hachimangū Shrine]][[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsushima, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Tsushima Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsushima, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]]
